purpose: Per-game SPLITROOT unit ledger: roster slots, stats, mechanics, team use, shop-tech gating, and current visual asset selections.
claim_boundary: Prototype stats are gameplay contracts for iteration, not final balance commitments.
A visual asset is only in-game when a local status, load log, proof artifact, or captured frame says so.
When a local C++ value conflicts with an older manifest note, this ledger records the current C++ value and keeps the conflict visible.

mechanics: Unlimited practical quiver in current code; fire cycle is the actual rhythm gate.
Straight living-arrow projectile, visible and dodgeable after the current speed reduction.
Free basic item keeps a razed team able to fight.
Single pressure bolt with crank reload pacing.
Projectile speed is intentionally under the no-gunpowder hitscan-feel ceiling.
Lenswright presentation must read as pressure/clockwork/optics, never firearm or powder.

team_tech_buildings.team_use: Opens pressure-bolt ranged play and the first Lenswright body row. If destroyed, those rows lock again.
Opens Kinwild pack pressure: a hunter body and a companion/scout row. This is the first fix for the old Kinwild no-units gap.
Opens Verdant body/item growth. Current row is a placeholder runner until a stronger organic Verdant asset lands.

global_asap.name: Infinity Blade Collection
global_asap.intended_use: Weapons, warriors, adversaries, effects, and faction-flavored shop visuals once entitlement/download is available.

verdant_choir.name: Paragon: The Fey
verdant_choir.use: Briar Saint, grove caster, Verdant magic/nature silhouette.

verdant_choir.name: Paragon: Morigesh
verdant_choir.use: Darker Verdant witch or sporecaster direction.

lenswright_compact.name: Paragon: Kwang
lenswright_compact.use: Disciplined melee body if Lenswright needs a close kit.

kinwild_covenant.name: Paragon: Khaimera
kinwild_covenant.use: Primary Kinwild hunter or beast-body candidate.

kinwild_covenant.name: Paragon: Rampage
kinwild_covenant.use: Late-tech creature tank or beast-form item.

factions.team_identity: Terrain control, living arrows, root-vault mobility, bramble cover, grove healing, agile defenders.
factions.silhouette_contract: Vertical bias, rounded organic cap, right-side quiver/bramble protrusion, 1.05x capsule height.

shop.runtime_override_note: Runtime proof flag ArchonRunRuntimeRallyProof lowers reinforcement cost to 5 supply; default match config and shop catalog still show 150.

mechanics: Frontline melee escort.
Bramble guard/cover read.
Always-available basic body/squad so a losing team is not fully locked out.
Verdant-controlled body should use VerdantRootVault when a playable body version exists.
units.team_use: Cheap durable screen that holds lanes, escorts ranged units, and buys time for grove or hero play.

asset_selection.notes: Needs an organic melee body or armored thorn infantry asset.

mechanics: Spore bolt harassment.
Spore bloom area support.
Softens clustered enemies and supports Thornbound pushes.
units.team_use: Backline damage and area pressure behind Thornbound or grove cover.

asset_selection.notes: Morigesh is the best approved visual direction for a darker spore/witch read.

mechanics: Vine lash.
Growth pulse heal or cover sustain.
Grow cover patch for RTS/FPS lane shaping.
units.team_use: Sustain and terrain control anchor for teams that invested in Grove.

asset_selection.notes: The Fey is the strongest approved source for grove caster presentation.

mechanics: Placeholder agile Verdant body for proving tech-gated unit purchases.
Should eventually collapse into a real Thornbound, Sporecaster, Grovekeeper, or Verdant scout row.
units.team_use: Short-term Grove unlock that proves the shop can sell a Verdant unit while better assets are sourced.

asset_selection.notes: AI-generated and zero reviews; use only as a temporary proof body, not faction identity.

mechanics: Thorn sweep / root snare lineage from the v0 unit sheet.
BriarWall: 6m wall, 200HP, 12s, vault-able by Verdant root-vault.
ChoirCircle: 12m radius, allies heal 5HP/s, enemies take 4 damage/s, 8s duration, 60s cooldown.
Paid hero discipline: horizontal variety only, no raw power sale.
units.team_use: Late unlock Verdant anchor for sustain, cover, and decisive terrain control without breaking the horizontal-only hero rule.

asset_selection.notes: The Fey is the current best approved source candidate.

factions.team_identity: Pack pressure, bonded beasts, scouting, shock assaults, shapeshift bursts, bound-leap mobility.
factions.silhouette_contract: Forward-leaning hunter with cloak mantle and animal companion at the right hip.

mechanics: Bound-leap mobility: 650 forward impulse, 400 vertical impulse, 0.33 stamina cost.
Bounding gait target speed 1150 while stamina allows.
Spear pressure and beast-pact melee identity.
Designed to close distance, flank ranged units, and punish isolated tech buildings.
units.team_use: First real Kinwild body row. Gives Beast Den a readable offensive payoff and fixes the previous no-Kinwild-units gap.

asset_selection.notes: Best current Kinwild body candidate; needs safe UE import and animation/rig verification.

mechanics: Companion silhouette reinforces Kinwild identity at distance.
Scout, reveal, chase, or bite-assist role depending on next implementation slice.
Pairs naturally with Spear Hunter for pack pressure.
units.team_use: Cheap scouting and harassment body from Beast Den; helps Kinwild feel like a pack faction immediately.

asset_selection.notes: Useful gameplay-readable companion prototype; style may be too low-poly for final AAA target.

mechanics: Hunt horn / pack aura slot implied by Kinwild audio palette.
Should boost pack cohesion, reveal scent trails, or call a temporary beast pressure wave.
Needs connector-backed spec before implementation.
units.team_use: Would give Kinwild a support/hero identity beyond raw melee pressure.

asset_selection.notes: This is the most important Kinwild roster design gap after Spear Hunter and Companion.

factions.team_identity: Pre-gunpowder science: pressure crossbows, clockwork, optics, field works, alchemy, siege tools.
factions.silhouette_contract: Wide anchored base, right-shoulder crossbow protrusion, reflective head-lens sphere. No gunpowder.

mechanics: Impact baton or pressure punch.
Brace shield.
Frontline holder for Lenswright ranged units.
units.team_use: Bodyguard screen for Bracewright/Arbalist lines and field works.

asset_selection.notes: Greystone or Terra are the best approved heavy-kit candidates.

mechanics: Defensive ranged AI.
Single pressure-bolt shot, 1.5s fire cycle, 2.2s reload.
Seeks cover under current AI combat policy thresholds.
No gunpowder, no firearms, no muzzle flashes.
units.team_use: Armory payoff for a team that wants disciplined ranged pressure and cover-holding defense.

asset_selection.notes: C++ actor currently uses debug basic-shape mesh; Rogue is the selected shop/body candidate for first visual swap.

mechanics: ScoutNoWeapon AI role.
Long-vision optic unit with no ranged weapon at v0.
Should feed map-table information and enemy approach reads.
units.team_use: Information advantage unit for Armory teams; lets Lenswright play around visibility and defensive preparation.

asset_selection.notes: Needs optic head/spyglass prop treatment; Rogue may cover body only.

mechanics: Lens-guided bolt.
Focus shot.
Could merge with Bracewright or become a higher-damage arbalist variant.
units.team_use: If kept separate, this becomes the damage counterpart to the defensive Bracewright.

asset_selection.notes: Needs a decision: keep as a separate unit or fold into Bracewright.

mechanics: Repair tool.
Wrench or spark prod self-defense.
Deploy pressure node or field-work support device.
units.team_use: Keeps Lenswright buildings and siege tools alive; gives the team an engineering economy identity.

asset_selection.notes: Needs tool/pack silhouette, not just a generic human body.

mechanics: Prototype Lenswright shop body for proving unit purchases.
Should eventually express pressure-thrust mobility once that faction verb is contracted.
Can act as scout/skirmisher until Sundial and Bracewright visual swaps land.
units.team_use: Fast Armory unit row for teams that want map pressure instead of only a weapon unlock.

asset_selection.notes: Best currently imported non-AI humanoid candidate for first Lenswright body work.

stats.reconcile_note: Current local asset manifest names abilities but not a newer locked health value; old v0 unit sheet value 280 is retained here pending connector reconciliation.

mechanics: Pressure lance.
Deploy device/turret lineage.
Repair pulse.
PressureGate deploy: pad doubles pressure-thrust boost until destroyed.
OpticBarrage: 10m circle, 5 pressure-bolts over 4s, 35 damage each, explicitly not gunpowder.
units.team_use: Late unlock siege/control hero for teams investing in Lenswright field works and pressure technology.

asset_selection.notes: Needs heavy artificer silhouette plus no-gunpowder VFX audit.

mechanics: Team supply buys bodies through TryPurchaseReinforcement.
Runtime fielding spawns bots and logs ReinforcementPurchased/ReinforcementFielded.
TrainUnit RTS intent targets verdant_reinforcement_squad in current input bridge.
squad_packages.team_use: Basic comeback-proof army fill. It keeps teams participating even when advanced tech rows are gone.

asset_selection.notes: Should eventually be faction-aware instead of always targeting verdant_reinforcement_squad.

squad_packages.team_use: Cheap line holder and first-60-seconds table command proof lineage.
Small ranged support group behind Thornbound.
Self-sustaining Verdant push package after Grove.
Shield line for pressure-bolt units and field works.
Ranged Lenswright pressure package; may remap to Bracewright in current code.
Durable push column with repair and pressure-node support.
First Kinwild RTS package: fast flank, scout, chase, and tech-building harassment.

open_roster_decisions.decision_needed: Keep Lens Arbalist as a separate damage unit, or make Bracewright the canonical ranged basic.
open_roster_decisions.why_it_matters: Avoid two nearly identical Lenswright crossbow bodies competing for asset and shop rows.

open_roster_decisions.decision_needed: Decide whether the temporary runner becomes a true Verdant scout, folds into Thornbound, or gets replaced by Sporecaster/Grovekeeper.
open_roster_decisions.why_it_matters: The selected AI-generated placeholder should not become a faction identity anchor by inertia.

open_roster_decisions.decision_needed: Author the Kinwild support/hero slot around pack aura, scent reveal, or beast call.
open_roster_decisions.why_it_matters: Kinwild needs more than two Beast Den rows to stand as a complete faction.

open_roster_decisions.decision_needed: Make reinforcement training faction-aware instead of always targeting verdant_reinforcement_squad.
open_roster_decisions.why_it_matters: The shop and unit ledger now cover three factions; reinforcement intent still carries an early Verdant target id.
